summary: External-reviewer improvement cycle addressing berendt's review #1. Three changes: (1) push-keystone-keys.hcl gained a header comment explaining that `read` is retained for policy-portability and is redundant at the current binding (I-001); (2) setup-auth.sh loop refactored to initialise token_policies from the read-only baseline and conditionally append `push-keystone-keys`, guarding against future branch-addition mistakes under set -u (I-002); (3) chainsaw basic-deployment catch block now also dumps `kubectl -n openbao logs --tail=100` so 403 regressions can be diagnosed from a single CI run without a second pass (W-001). Metadata also updated: progress.json phase flipped to 'improving' and a CC-0083 entry appended to the require-explicit-failure-branch pattern file. The in-scope production files keep the BLOCKER guarantees from review #1 intact: two literal paths only, capabilities exactly ["create", "update", "read"], push-keystone-keys bound only on management, eso-management.hcl untouched by this diff.

tests_checklist.item: Chainsaw assertion step covers both PushSecrets with Ready=True
Catch block captures ESO + OpenBao logs for single-run diagnosis
No new unit tests required — changes are declarative (HCL, shell config, chainsaw YAML)
Live-cluster run to confirm chainsaw passes

code_quality_checklist.item: Follows existing push-*.hcl policy file structure
Refactored loop preserves idempotence and log() tone
Consistent SPDX headers and CC-0083 feature references
No scope creep — only W-001/I-001/I-002 addressed

security_checklist.item: Two literal paths, no wildcard (least privilege preserved)
Capabilities restricted to create/update/read — no delete, no list
push-keystone-keys bound only on management ESO role
eso-management.hcl stays read-only (not in this diff)
No secrets in added kubectl log commands (catch block reads controller logs, not secret contents)

architecture_checklist.item: Loop refactor hardens for future cluster additions without over-engineering

dry_yagni_checklist.item: I-003 (declarative map) explicitly deferred as premature — appropriate YAGNI call

fail_fast_checklist.item: Baseline assignment eliminates silent state-leak failure mode flagged in I-002

defensive_checklist.item: token_policies initialised every iteration — no reliance on prior branch execution
Catch block uses `|| true` on every kubectl to avoid masking real failure with diagnostic failure

issues_found.description: The CC-0083 entry appended to .planwerk/review_patterns/require-explicit-failure-branch-in-test-passfail-logic.md (lines 33-36) is thematically different from the pattern it lives under. The pattern file is explicitly about `if ... PASS=$((PASS + 1)) ... fi` logic without a FAIL branch in shell tests; the new CC-0083 entry is about missing OpenBao logs in a chainsaw catch block (a diagnostics-in-failure-handlers concern, not a pass/fail counter concern). Detection-Hint on line 4 still describes only the PASS-counter case, so a future reviewer searching by detection-hint will not discover the CC-0083 guidance. Not code-correctness — a taxonomy nit on a review-pattern file — but it will erode the signal of the pattern index over time.
issues_found.fix: Either (a) move the CC-0083 entry into a new review_pattern file whose title/detection-hint actually matches the guidance (e.g., 'capture-authoritative-component-logs-in-test-catch-blocks.md'), or (b) broaden this pattern's Detection-Hint to explicitly include 'test failure handlers lacking logs from the authoritative component enforcing the policy' alongside the existing PASS/FAIL case.
issues_found.evidence: Line 4 reads: 'Look for if-blocks that increment PASS but have no corresponding else/elif that increments FAIL.' — unchanged. Line 33 adds a CC-0083 entry whose feedback is 'A 403 on PushSecret is most authoritatively diagnosed from OpenBao's audit log ... Without OpenBao logs in the catch block, operators need a second CI run.' — unrelated to PASS/FAIL counters.

suggested_improvements: Split the cross-cutting 'capture authoritative component logs in catch blocks' guidance into its own review_pattern file so future reviewers can discover it by detection-hint rather than by reading occurrences of an unrelated pattern.

When I-003 finally ships (e.g., a second push-* policy ever needs management-only binding), consider the declarative EXTRA_POLICIES map the external reviewer suggested — the current if/management branch is appropriate for one extra, but grows awkwardly with more.
next_steps: Re-verify on a live kind cluster: apply deploy-infra, reconcile basic Keystone CR, confirm both PushSecrets reach Ready=True and `bao kv get .../fernet-keys` + `.../credential-keys` return non-empty data.

(Optional) Re-run the I-002 regression validation: remove push-keystone-keys.hcl locally, re-bootstrap, re-run chainsaw basic-deployment, confirm the OpenBao logs in the catch now surface the 403 path + policy denial (this is the value-add the W-001 fix was supposed to unlock).
